Title: Synthesis of Very-Long-Chain Fatty Acids in the Epidermis Controls Plant Organ Growth by Restricting Cell Proliferation

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: L1 layer/epidermis of SAM, young leaves and inflorescence stem; also detected in embryos, cotyledons, true leaves, pistils and anthers by promoter:GUS
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly epidermis/L1; faint signal in vasculature with translational fusion
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cuticular wax formation and non-cell-autonomous restriction of cytokinin biosynthesis/cell proliferation during shoot development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Epidermal PAS2-dependent VLCFA synthesis suppresses IPT3 expression and cytokinin accumulation in the vasculature, thereby restricting cell proliferation; reduced PAS2 causes vasculature/RZ overproliferation and abnormal growth
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Leaky mutant analysis (pas2-1); epidermis-specific RNAi; epidermis-specific complementation; procambium-specific expression tests; hormone quantification; reporter assays (ProIPT3:GUS, ProARR6:GUS, ProCDKB2;1:NT-GUS); microarray
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAS2 is expressed mainly in the epidermal L1 layer. 2. Epidermis-specific PAS2 knockdown phenocopies pas2-1, while epidermis-specific PAS2 complementation rescues pas2-1. 3. Reduced PAS2/VLCFA synthesis elevates IPT3 expression and cytokinin levels, enhancing cell proliferation in vasculature and rib zone.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CDKA (reported background interaction as antiphosphatase, but phenotype interpreted here mainly through VLCFA synthesis)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cafenstrole treatment (30 nM mild, 3 μM strong inhibitor of VLCFA elongation)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe reduction causes impaired cuticle formation, leaf fusion, swollen hypocotyls, severe growth retardation and embryo lethality in loss-of-function background
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Modulating epidermal VLCFA synthesis may alter organ size through cytokinin-mediated proliferation, but strong suppression is deleterious due to cuticle defects; mild tuning could affect biomass/leaf size
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because PAS2 is an elongase-complex structural enzyme not directly extracted here as the focal terminal nutrient-product determinant; study emphasizes epidermis-to-vasculature signaling role.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically driven in epidermis or vasculature depending on promoter
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cytokinin (active forms including iP and tZ)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cell proliferation and shoot organ growth
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Vasculature and expanded ectopic domain under low-VLCFA conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Vasculature-specific CKX1 degrades cytokinin and suppresses cafenstrole-induced leaf enlargement and vascular overproliferation; epidermis-specific CKX1 does not
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tissue-specific transgenic expression of CKX1–Venus; leaf size/cell number measurements; histological analysis in cafenstrole-treated plants and pas2-1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ATHB8-driven CKX1 suppresses low-VLCFA-induced overproliferation and leaf enlargement. 2. ATML1-driven CKX1 fails to suppress these phenotypes. 3. Cytokinin increase in vasculature is therefore a major cause of overproliferation under reduced VLCFA synthesis.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In pas2-1, vasculature-specific CKX1 only partially suppresses microscopic defects and does not rescue gross morphology because severe cuticle defects remain
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tissue-specific cytokinin depletion to counter excessive proliferation caused by altered lipid metabolism; may help decouple growth from hormonal imbalance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as causal suppression tool rather than native upstream determinant.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting enzyme for cytokinin biosynthesis as stated in paper
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Adenosine phosphates and isoprenoid donor for isopentenyl transfer
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first and rate-limiting isopentenyl transfer step producing iPRPs in cytokinin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies committed precursor pool for active cytokinins iP and tZ
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In low-VLCFA conditions expression intensity increases in vascular bundles and expands into spongy mesophyll cells
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cell division and leaf enlargement
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Vasculature; ectopic mesophyll expression domain under low-VLCFA conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased IPT3 expression under reduced VLCFA synthesis elevates cytokinin biosynthesis and drives overproliferation; ipt3;5;7 suppresses cafenstrole-induced leaf enlargement
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray; qRT-PCR; ProIPT3:GUS reporter; time-course after cafenstrole; genetic suppression using ipt3;5;7 triple mutant; cytokinin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IPT3 transcripts are strongly elevated in pas2-1 and by cafenstrole treatment. 2. IPT3 induction precedes increased active cytokinin and overproliferation. 3. Reducing IPT activity in ipt3;5;7 suppresses low-VLCFA-induced leaf enlargement and increased cell number.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: IPT5; IPT7 (genetic redundancy in triple mutant)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-VLCFA conditions caused by pas2-1 or cafenstrole
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher IPT3/cytokinin can cause ectopic proliferation and disorganized vasculature
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A key leverage point to modulate cytokinin-driven organ size, but overactivation risks tissue disorganization; should be controlled spatially
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central mediator linking epidermal VLCFA status to internal cell proliferation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Conversion from iP-type precursors to tZ-type precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts isopentenyladenine riboside phosphates to trans-zeatin riboside phosphates
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor toward tZ-type cytokinins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript level increased in pas2-1 by microarray
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cell proliferation and shoot growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated under low-VLCFA conditions, likely contributing to increased tZ biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP735A2 transcript is elevated 6.6-fold in pas2-1. 2. The increase is consistent with elevated tZ-related cytokinin species under low-VLCFA conditions. 3. It is proposed as an additional target contributing to cytokinin overproduction.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible secondary target for controlling tZ-type cytokinin increase accompanying altered lipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in Results as induced gene, but direct functional suppression was not tested in this paper.
